utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good morning noah tell me a little bit about why you are here today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi document i'm i'm just had you know the back pain you know and i've i've had it for you know about a week and

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: oh

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know i mean i tried some you know i mean medications but you know some over the counter stuff but it does n't go away

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay can you tell me a little bit more about it how did it start or how did it do you remember injuring yourself

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no not really i mean i was just like you know i guess i one day i mean i just twisted you know on my left and maybe i mean i might have picked something up

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i mean at that time i mean there was this pain but i you know it went away you know and i thought that was you know that was you know the end of it but you know i mean after you know a few hours you know it's it kinda it came back

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been there since then you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and is it anywhere in specific is it in the middle of the back across the whole low back or on one side or the other

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think it's more towards you know right in the middle maybe on the lower side and nothing on the you know i mean it sometimes it goes back you know maybe having troubles with the left eye i suppose but i mean it's aggravated you know when i you know when i do some you know maybe i'm a sudden movement to the left

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and that's when you get the pain more maybe located in the center but it goes across to the little back and i on the left side and i also heard you say you know if you twist suddenly that makes it worse is that correct

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it makes it worse it makes it worse i mean yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: now you know i was looking at your records and i know you have that history of diabetes and i have n't seen you a while for a follow-up for that how you've been feeling

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean been okay i mean i take my meds

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i try to still have sugar but you know i mean i you know you know i mean whenever i get the chance i mean i you know i exercise but you know i mean that's about it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so you're on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i think i think you're on that metformin twice a day

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you test your blood sugar at home in the mornings

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i mean i try to

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: most of the morning but sometimes i mean i go skiing yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how have those numbers been

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they bumped towards i mean bit towards the higher side i mean i must have been

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well if

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah if i remember right your family do n't they own that that bakery down on the corner of main street and did n't they own that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how do you do you stay away from those i i got ta be honest i love i love the cakes and donuts they make those are delicious

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i try to

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but i mean you know they they you know i have to visit them and you know i end up you know i mean getting some stuff but it's not that much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but it's been i try to contain it you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well let me so we're gon na have to watch that and i'll come back and we'll talk a little bit about the diabetes but let's go ahead and do that i do n't wan na do a a a quick physical exam on you i'm gon na look in your eyes here and so let me quickly look in your ears

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i see

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that looks good and any let me let me listen to your lungs okay your lungs are clear and your heart it's a regular rate and rhythm that's all good as we go through that now i want you to sit up here i'm gon na press down your back starting at the top and any pain up top no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then once i get down here and around that low back i wan na press in the center first does that cause you a lot of pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that that's the spot

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and if i come over here to the right no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not that much you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so and then on the left hand side if i push down here kind of into your butt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lay back on the stretcher for me for a second i'm gon na go ahead and and lift your legs for me or i'm gon na go ahead and lift your legs for you just relax i'm gon na start here on the right side when i lift that right leg up does that cause you significant pain or any pain at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little a little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay let me come around let me lift up the left side if i lift that up does that cause

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah yeah that's that's

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: still get tender yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i can see that gritts on your face so okay that's good and you know as i look at your lower extremities there is no pain or numbness or tingling in your feet or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay so lem me talk a little bit about my impression and plan so for that low back pain i believe you have a musculoskeletal low back strain i'm going to order a low back x-ray so we'll order that and get that done as an outpatient i do n't expect to see anything significant but it's gon na tell me how those joint spaces look and if you know if we're dealing with any disk issue but that will be the start of it and i'm also gon na order some physical therapy two for two reasons to help with a little bit with the pain but also strengthen some of the muscles there in the back so those are the two things that we are gon na do now i want you to take take four hundred milligrams of ibuprofen you can take that occasionally with some tylenol when the pain gets more severe up to about three times a day i'm gon na also give you a prescription for some flexeril that's a muscle relaxant and i want you to take one of those once a day every evening but be careful if you're doing any strenuous or hazardous activities such as driving after you take those now for your diabetes my impression is that your diabetes is is under control i noticed that we do n't have n't had a a1c for almost five months so i'm also gon na order an a1c so we can find out where we are with that continue with your diet and exercise we know that's really important and then continue with your metformin i'm not gon na make any changes today but we'll go ahead and look at that once i get that results of that a1c do you have any questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah can i still go to the bakery

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i would yes it's nice for you to go to the bakery but you ca n't have any donuts i'm no i you just really need to watch that intake of of anything that you might get at the at the bakery so just just be careful with those carbohydrates those sweets that you're taking as we go through your day it's occasional occasional one perfectly fine but on a routine basis we need to watch that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right and how soon should i you know check my blood sugars i mean what's a good you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i that's a great question i'm really glad you asked that i would prefer that you're taking your blood pressure every morning the first thing you get up when you get up in the morning that's gon na give me a good idea and so if you can do that the first thing in the morning and keep a record of it you know i know your glucometer keeps a record of it but if you can write them down and once we get that a1c that's gon na give me a little more guidance on how to deal with your blood your diabetes

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right do you think would i need to see you know a pain doctor or you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: not yet let's let's get the that's a great question i may end up referring you to a a pain doctor for that low back pain but i really would like to start with some of the diagnostics first let's get an x-ray and if there is you know any question of that x-ray and the pain continues i'm gon na go ahead and maybe order a cat scan and then at that point in time we can refer you to a pain doctor if we're still having significant pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay thank you i'm gon na send my nursing to see you and we'll get you out the door

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: take care

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that one is a follow-up mm-hmm

detail_list.summary: Back pain
The patient reports back pain for one week.
The patient tried over-the-counter medications without relief.
The doctor asks about the onset of pain and potential injury.
The patient reports twisting to the left and possibly lifting something.
The doctor asks for the specific location of the back pain.
The patient reports pain in the midline of the lower back, aggravated by sudden movements to the left.
Diabetes
The patient reports taking his diabetes medication.
The patient takes metformin twice daily.
The doctor asks if the patient tests blood sugar in the mornings.
The patient reports blood sugar levels are on the higher side.
The patient's family owns a bakery.
The doctor performs a physical exam including eyes, ears, lungs, and heart, all of which are normal.
The patient has no pain to palpation of the upper back.
The patient has pain to palpation of the midline of the lower back.
The patient has no pain to palpation of the right lower back.
The patient has pain to palpation of the left lower back.
The patient has a mildly positive straight leg raise on the right.
The patient has a positive straight leg raise on the left.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in the feet.
The doctor orders a low back x-ray and physical therapy.
The patient is instructed to take 400 mg of ibuprofen up to 3 times daily and is prescribed Flexeril once daily in the evening.
The doctor orders an A1c test and instructs the patient to continue metformin, diet, and exercise.
The patient asks how often he should check his blood sugar.
The doctor instructs the patient to check blood sugar every morning and keep a log.
The patient asks if he needs to see a pain specialist.
The doctor states a referral to a pain specialist may be considered if diagnostics do not resolve the issue.
